prompt: A traditional Japanese sumi-e ink wash painting on aged rice paper. A solitary heron standing in shallow water amid a few delicate reeds, looking down. Bold confident brush strokes for the heron body in deep black ink, soft gray washes for water reflection and distant mist. A single small red hanko seal in the lower-right corner. Visible paper texture and ink bleed. Minimalist composition with abundant negative space, classical East Asian ink painting tradition.
